core_principles: Semantic renderer only: Do not calculate states, use API data as truth
Visual hierarchy is strict: Inconsistent > Empty > Validated > Corrected > Partial > Complete > Not Expected
Progressive disclosure: Hover -> Detail, Click -> Drill-down
No decorative elements: Functional, dense, auditable UI

rules.numbers: Use tabular-nums for all calendar dates and summary metrics to ensure alignment
rules.hierarchy: Max H1 size text-2xl or text-3xl. Mostly text-sm to text-base for dense operational data

desktop.calendar: grid grid-cols-7 gap-0 border-l border-t border-gray-200 (dense grid, table-like). Minimum height for cells (e.g., min-h-[120px]).
desktop.summary: Bento 'Control Room' grid at the top. grid-cols-4 or flex-wrap, highly compact, dense data.
desktop.drill_down: Slide-over (Sheet) or Modal (Dialog) appearing on day click. Do not expand the cell itself to avoid breaking grid.

mobile.calendar: Vertical flex list. MUST BE SORTED BY SEVERITY (Inconsistent -> Empty -> Validated -> Corrected -> Partial -> Complete). 'Not expected' should be collapsed or hidden entirely.
mobile.summary: Compact stacked cards pinned to top.

components_strategy.SummaryBar: Persistent top bar. 'X slots esperados | Y completos | Z pendentes | W inconsistências'. Bold metrics.
components_strategy.Filters: Select components for Escola, Turma, Range de datas (default: current month).
components_strategy.CalendarDayCell: Contains date number (top right), max 2-3 indicators/badges (bottom/left). Must use group-hover for the mini-tooltip.
components_strategy.MiniTooltip: Absolute or Popover showing 'status do dia, entries esperadas, quem está pendente'.
components_strategy.OrphanEvidenceList: Separate list/table section outside the calendar grid for auditing launches outside expected slots.
components_strategy.Legend: ALWAYS VISIBLE (flex flex-wrap gap-2 text-xs) near the top. Never hide behind an icon.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build strictly in React (.js files, no TSX) using Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui components (if needed, but custom HTML/CSS is better for the dense calendar grid).
Use 'IBM Plex Sans' (or system tabular fonts) for rendering.
DO NOT implement real-time websockets or heavy charts. Focus on pure HTML/CSS layout.
MOCK the endpoint 'GET /api/calendar/diary-state/{class_id}?from=&to=' using the exact JSON shape provided in the problem statement.
The UI is a DUMB semantic renderer. Map the 'status' directly to the 'color_system.semantic_states' defined here.
Ensure 'not_expected' days are visually deprioritized (low opacity, no badges, no strong borders).
Implement the Desktop vs Mobile difference: Desktop = 30-day grid. Mobile = severity-ordered vertical list.
Ensure all interactive elements have data-testid attributes.
